reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

sources.title: HTML form element
sources.versionScope: Current HTML standard and evergreen browsers

claims.statement: An HTML form represents a section containing controls for submitting information.
The form action and method influence where and how native form submission sends data.
JavaScript-handled forms and server-submitted forms have different request behavior.

verifications.method: Submit known valid inputs and confirm the intended handler or request receives them.
verifications.expectedResult: The intended handler or request receives them.

verifications.method: Submit an empty or invalid value and confirm the failure path is understandable.
Check that labels and input names match the values read by JavaScript or the server.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether form behaves as documented: Check that labels and input names match the values read by JavaScript or the server.

limitations: A form can submit natively or have its submit event handled by JavaScript.
Client-side validation improves feedback but does not replace server-side validation for untrusted input.
Only successful named controls contribute to normal form submission.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
